title: local-service-endpoint-v1
description: Typed endpoint descriptor for local service communication. Producer: a tool that wants to advertise a local socket or TCP endpoint for system-to-system calls. Consumer: downstream tools that need to locate and connect to a capability-serving endpoint. Unlike capability-runtime-lease-v1 (which is ephemeral and PID-bound), this descriptor is static and suitable for config files, registries, or stored endpoint mappings. Transport is unix-socket, tcp, or http.

transport.description: Transport kind for this endpoint. 'unix-socket': local Unix domain socket at endpoint path. 'tcp': TCP listen on host:port. 'http': HTTP endpoint at http://host:port.

endpoint.description: Endpoint address. For 'unix-socket': absolute socket path (e.g. '/tmp/hyphae.sock'). For 'tcp': 'host:port' string (e.g. '127.0.0.1:9000'). For 'http': full URL (e.g. 'http://127.0.0.1:9000').

capability_id.description: Optional capability identifier this endpoint serves (e.g. 'memory.store.v1'). Allows a registry to match endpoints to capability requests.

timeout_ms.description: Optional default call timeout in milliseconds for this endpoint. Consumers should use this as the hint for per-call timeouts unless overridden.

health_probe.description: Optional health probe configuration for this endpoint.

method.description: Health check method (e.g. 'GET /health', 'POST /healthz'). Format is transport-specific.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout for the health probe in milliseconds. If omitted, uses the endpoint-level timeout_ms.

version.description: Optional version string of the server at this endpoint (e.g. '1.4.2'). Used to detect version mismatch or compatibility issues.
